@@ -8,58 +8,105 @@ enum AppStatus: Equatable {
     case cleaningUp
     case error(String)
 
-    var menuTitle: String {
+    var statusLabel: String {
         switch self {
         case .loading:
-            return "Loading models..."
+            return "Loading model..."
         case .ready:
             return "Ready"
+        case .recording:
+            return "Recording"
+        case .transcribing:
+            return "Transcribing"
+        case .cleaningUp:
+            return "Cleaning up"
+        case .error:
+            return "Error"
+        }
+    }
+
+    var menuActivityTitle: String? {
+        switch self {
+        case .loading:
+            return "Loading..."
         case .recording:
             return "Recording..."
         case .transcribing:
             return "Transcribing..."
         case .cleaningUp:
             return "Cleaning up..."
-        case let .error(message):
-            return message
+        case .ready, .error:
+            return nil
         }
     }
+
+    var menuTitle: String {
+        statusLabel
+    }
 }
 
 enum StatusMenuIcon {
     static func makeImage(for status: AppStatus) -> NSImage {
-        let image = NSImage(size: NSSize(width: 18, height: 18))
-        image.lockFocus()
-
-        let dotRect = NSRect(x: 4, y: 4, width: 10, height: 10)
-        let dotPath = NSBezierPath(ovalIn: dotRect)
-        iconColor(for: status).setFill()
-        dotPath.fill()
-
-        image.unlockFocus()
-        image.isTemplate = usesTemplateRendering(for: status)
-        return image
-    }
-
-    private static func iconColor(for status: AppStatus) -> NSColor {
         switch status {
         case .loading:
-            return .systemOrange
-        case .recording:
-            return .systemRed
+            return tintedSystemSymbol(
+                name: "ellipsis.circle.fill",
+                color: .systemOrange
+            )
         case .error:
-            return .systemYellow
+            return tintedSystemSymbol(
+                name: "exclamationmark.triangle.fill",
+                color: .systemYellow
+            )
+        case .recording:
+            return pepperImage(color: .systemRed, template: false)
         case .ready, .transcribing, .cleaningUp:
-            return .labelColor
+            return pepperImage(color: .labelColor, template: true)
         }
     }
 
-    private static func usesTemplateRendering(for status: AppStatus) -> Bool {
-        switch status {
-        case .recording:
-            return false
-        case .loading, .ready, .transcribing, .cleaningUp, .error:
-            return true
-        }
+    private static func tintedSystemSymbol(name: String, color: NSColor) -> NSImage {
+        let image = NSImage(
+            systemSymbolName: name,
+            accessibilityDescription: "Ghost Pepper status"
+        ) ?? NSImage(size: NSSize(width: 18, height: 18))
+        let rendered = image.withSymbolConfiguration(
+            NSImage.SymbolConfiguration(pointSize: 15, weight: .semibold)
+        ) ?? image
+        rendered.isTemplate = false
+        rendered.lockFocus()
+        color.set()
+        let rect = NSRect(origin: .zero, size: rendered.size)
+        rect.fill(using: .sourceAtop)
+        rendered.unlockFocus()
+        return rendered
+    }
+
+    private static func pepperImage(color: NSColor, template: Bool) -> NSImage {
+        let size = NSSize(width: 18, height: 18)
+        let image = NSImage(size: size)
+        image.lockFocus()
+        color.setFill()
+
+        let body = NSBezierPath()
+        body.move(to: NSPoint(x: 8.5, y: 2.0))
+        body.curve(to: NSPoint(x: 5.0, y: 10.2), controlPoint1: NSPoint(x: 5.8, y: 3.5), controlPoint2: NSPoint(x: 3.8, y: 6.3))
+        body.curve(to: NSPoint(x: 10.4, y: 15.2), controlPoint1: NSPoint(x: 5.0, y: 13.2), controlPoint2: NSPoint(x: 7.7, y: 15.7))
+        body.curve(to: NSPoint(x: 13.4, y: 8.7), controlPoint1: NSPoint(x: 13.6, y: 14.1), controlPoint2: NSPoint(x: 14.7, y: 10.7))
+        body.curve(to: NSPoint(x: 8.5, y: 2.0), controlPoint1: NSPoint(x: 12.8, y: 5.7), controlPoint2: NSPoint(x: 10.7, y: 3.4))
+        body.close()
+        body.fill()
+
+        let stem = NSBezierPath()
+        stem.move(to: NSPoint(x: 10.5, y: 14.8))
+        stem.curve(to: NSPoint(x: 12.8, y: 16.5), controlPoint1: NSPoint(x: 11.0, y: 15.9), controlPoint2: NSPoint(x: 12.0, y: 16.6))
+        stem.curve(to: NSPoint(x: 12.7, y: 14.4), controlPoint1: NSPoint(x: 13.5, y: 16.4), controlPoint2: NSPoint(x: 13.5, y: 15.0))
+        stem.curve(to: NSPoint(x: 10.5, y: 14.8), controlPoint1: NSPoint(x: 12.0, y: 14.0), controlPoint2: NSPoint(x: 11.0, y: 14.1))
+        stem.close()
+        stem.fill()
+
+        image.unlockFocus()
+        image.isTemplate = template
+        return image
     }
 }

@@ -0,0 +1,184 @@
+import AppKit
+import XCTest
+@testable import GhostPepper
+
+@MainActor
+final class AppShellSupportTests: XCTestCase {
+    func testLazyUpdateCheckerBuildsDriverOnFirstCheckOnly() {
+        let factory = RecordingUpdateDriverFactory()
+        let checker = LazyUpdateChecker {
+            factory.makeDriver()
+        }
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(factory.makeCallCount, 0)
+
+        checker.checkForUpdates()
+        checker.checkForUpdates()
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(factory.makeCallCount, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(factory.driver.checkForUpdatesCallCount, 2)
+    }
+
+    func testApplicationTerminationCoordinatorCallsRegisteredCleaner() {
+        let cleaner = RecordingTerminationCleaner()
+        ApplicationTerminationCoordinator.setCleaner(cleaner)
+        addTeardownBlock {
+            ApplicationTerminationCoordinator.setCleaner(NoOpApplicationTerminationCleaner())
+        }
+
+        ApplicationTerminationCoordinator.handleWillTerminate()
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(cleaner.cancelScreenCapturePrefetchCallCount, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(cleaner.shutdownCleanupBackendCallCount, 1)
+    }
+
+    func testAppDelegateControlsAutomaticTerminationThroughLifecycleHooks() {
+        let automaticTerminationController = RecordingAutomaticTerminationController()
+        let cleaner = RecordingTerminationCleaner()
+        ApplicationTerminationCoordinator.setCleaner(cleaner)
+        addTeardownBlock {
+            ApplicationTerminationCoordinator.setCleaner(NoOpApplicationTerminationCleaner())
+        }
+        let appDelegate = AppDelegate(automaticTerminationController: automaticTerminationController)
+
+        appDelegate.applicationDidFinishLaunching(Notification(name: NSApplication.didFinishLaunchingNotification))
+        appDelegate.applicationWillTerminate(Notification(name: NSApplication.willTerminateNotification))
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            automaticTerminationController.disabledReasons,
+            ["Ghost Pepper keeps a persistent menu bar presence."]
+        )
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            automaticTerminationController.enabledReasons,
+            ["Ghost Pepper is shutting down."]
+        )
+        XCTAssertEqual(cleaner.cancelScreenCapturePrefetchCallCount, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(cleaner.shutdownCleanupBackendCallCount, 1)
+    }
+
+    func testDebugLogStoreReturnsEmptyEntriesWhenLogIsCorrupted() throws {
+        let logURL = makeTemporaryFileURL()
+        try Data("not-json".utf8).write(to: logURL)
+        let store = DebugLogStore(url: logURL)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(store.loadEntries(), [])
+    }
+
+    func testSettingsWindowManagerReusesWindowAfterCloseAttempt() throws {
+        let manager = SettingsWindowManager()
+        manager.show()
+        let firstWindow = try XCTUnwrap(manager.currentWindow)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(firstWindow.title, "Ghost Pepper Settings")
+        XCTAssertEqual(firstWindow.minSize, NSSize(width: 900, height: 680))
+        XCTAssertFalse(firstWindow.isReleasedWhenClosed)
+        XCTAssertFalse(manager.windowShouldClose(firstWindow))
+        XCTAssertFalse(firstWindow.isVisible)
+
+        manager.show()
+
+        XCTAssertTrue(manager.currentWindow === firstWindow)
+    }
+
+    func testDebugLogWindowManagerUsesFloatingUtilityPanel() throws {
+        let manager = DebugLogWindowManager(store: DebugLogStore(url: makeTemporaryFileURL()))
+        manager.show()
+        let panel = try XCTUnwrap(manager.currentWindow as? NSPanel)
+
+        XCTAssertTrue(panel.styleMask.contains(NSWindow.StyleMask.utilityWindow))
+        XCTAssertTrue(panel.isFloatingPanel)
+        XCTAssertFalse(panel.hidesOnDeactivate)
+        XCTAssertFalse(panel.isReleasedWhenClosed)
+        XCTAssertFalse(manager.windowShouldClose(panel))
+        XCTAssertFalse(panel.isVisible)
+
+        manager.show()
+
+        XCTAssertTrue(manager.currentWindow === panel)
+    }
+
+    func testOnboardingWindowManagerReleasesWindowAfterCompletion() throws {
+        let previousPolicy = NSApp.activationPolicy()
+        addTeardownBlock {
+            _ = NSApp.setActivationPolicy(previousPolicy)
+        }
+
+        _ = NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
+
+        let manager = OnboardingWindowManager()
+        var completionValue: Bool?
+        manager.onContinueToApp = { completionValue = $0 }
+
+        manager.show()
+        let firstWindow = try XCTUnwrap(manager.currentWindow)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(NSApp.activationPolicy(), .regular)
+
+        manager.finishOnboarding(permissionsAlreadyAcquired: true)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(completionValue, true)
+        XCTAssertNil(manager.currentWindow)
+        XCTAssertFalse(firstWindow.isVisible)
+
+        manager.show()
+
+        let secondWindow = try XCTUnwrap(manager.currentWindow)
+        XCTAssertFalse(secondWindow === firstWindow)
+    }
+
+    private func makeTemporaryFileURL() -> URL {
+        let directoryURL = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory
+            .appending(path: UUID().uuidString, directoryHint: .isDirectory)
+        try? FileManager.default.createDirectory(at: directoryURL, withIntermediateDirectories: true)
+        addTeardownBlock {
+            try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at: directoryURL)
+        }
+        return directoryURL.appending(path: "fixture.json", directoryHint: .notDirectory)
+    }
+}
+
+@MainActor
+private final class RecordingUpdateDriverFactory {
+    private(set) var makeCallCount = 0
+    let driver = RecordingUpdateDriver()
+
+    func makeDriver() -> any UpdateDriver {
+        makeCallCount += 1
+        return driver
+    }
+}
+
+@MainActor
+private final class RecordingUpdateDriver: UpdateDriver {
+    private(set) var checkForUpdatesCallCount = 0
+
+    func checkForUpdates() {
+        checkForUpdatesCallCount += 1
+    }
+}
+
+private final class RecordingTerminationCleaner: ApplicationTerminationCleaning, @unchecked Sendable {
+    private(set) var cancelScreenCapturePrefetchCallCount = 0
+    private(set) var shutdownCleanupBackendCallCount = 0
+
+    func cancelScreenCapturePrefetch() {
+        cancelScreenCapturePrefetchCallCount += 1
+    }
+
+    func shutdownCleanupBackend() {
+        shutdownCleanupBackendCallCount += 1
+    }
+}
+
+private final class RecordingAutomaticTerminationController: AutomaticTerminationControlling, @unchecked Sendable {
+    private(set) var disabledReasons: [String] = []
+    private(set) var enabledReasons: [String] = []
+
+    func disableAutomaticTermination(_ reason: String) {
+        disabledReasons.append(reason)
+    }
+
+    func enableAutomaticTermination(_ reason: String) {
+        enabledReasons.append(reason)
+    }
+}

@@ -94,6 +94,36 @@ final class TranscriptionLabArchiveStoreTests: XCTestCase {
         XCTAssertFalse(FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: rootURL.path()))
     }
 
+    func testCorruptArchiveDeletesIndexTimingsAndAudioDirectory() throws {
+        let rootURL = makeTemporaryRootURL()
+        let audioDirectoryURL = rootURL.appending(path: "audio", directoryHint: .isDirectory)
+        try FileManager.default.createDirectory(at: audioDirectoryURL, withIntermediateDirectories: true)
+        try Data("not-json".utf8).write(
+            to: rootURL.appending(path: "transcription-lab-index.json", directoryHint: .notDirectory)
+        )
+        try Data("also-not-json".utf8).write(
+            to: rootURL.appending(path: "transcription-lab-timings.json", directoryHint: .notDirectory)
+        )
+        try Data("audio".utf8).write(
+            to: audioDirectoryURL.appending(path: "clip.wav", directoryHint: .notDirectory)
+        )
+        let store = TranscriptionLabArchiveStore(rootURL: rootURL)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(store.loadEntries(), [])
+        XCTAssertEqual(store.loadTimings(), [:])
+        XCTAssertFalse(
+            FileManager.default.fileExists(
+                atPath: rootURL.appending(path: "transcription-lab-index.json", directoryHint: .notDirectory).path()
+            )
+        )
+        XCTAssertFalse(
+            FileManager.default.fileExists(
+                atPath: rootURL.appending(path: "transcription-lab-timings.json", directoryHint: .notDirectory).path()
+            )
+        )
+        XCTAssertFalse(FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: audioDirectoryURL.path()))
+    }
+
     private func makeTemporaryRootURL() -> URL {
         let rootURL = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory
             .appending(path: UUID().uuidString, directoryHint: .isDirectory)

@@ -20,3 +20,13 @@
 - Added hotkey monitor lifecycle controls for suspend/resume, clean stop, and idempotent start so shortcut capture can pause global input handling safely.
 - Wired successful paste snapshots into the post-paste learning seam, completed performance trace logging at paste end, and archived live recordings into the transcription-lab store as WAV plus JSON metadata/timings.
 **Summary:** Delivered the full accessibility paste path on top of the walking skeleton. Ghost Pepper now detects editable targets through direct accessibility queries plus a continuous fallback monitor, pastes via preserved clipboard + simulated Command+V when possible, falls back cleanly to copied text when not, logs a completed performance trace for delivered sessions, and archives each non-empty recording into the transcription-lab store for later replay.
+
+## ITER-0002
+
+**Completed:** 2026-04-09
+**Stories delivered:** STORY-0032, STORY-0033, STORY-0035, STORY-0037, STORY-0039, STORY-0041, STORY-0042, STORY-0043, STORY-0044, STORY-0045, STORY-0595, STORY-0596, STORY-0597, STORY-0598, STORY-0600, STORY-0602, STORY-0603, STORY-0604, STORY-0607, STORY-0608, STORY-0609, STORY-0835, STORY-0836, STORY-0837, STORY-0839, STORY-0843, STORY-0844, STORY-0845, STORY-0846, STORY-0847, STORY-0848, STORY-0849, STORY-0850, STORY-0851, STORY-0852, STORY-0853, STORY-0855, STORY-0856, STORY-0857
+**Tasks executed:**
+- Added permission-aware startup recovery for microphone, accessibility, input monitoring, and screen recording, including System Settings deep links and fail-closed Accessibility gating around AX-backed paste-target monitoring.
+- Built the menu-bar shell and lifecycle scaffolding: onboarding gating, reusable settings/debug/onboarding windows, launch-at-login registration and toggle control, lazy update checking, automatic-termination handling, and termination cleanup coordination seams.
+- Hardened shell-facing persistence and recovery behavior with new settings flags, debug-log corruption recovery, status/menu feedback coverage, and a test-harness fix that injects Accessibility trust into `PasteTargetMonitorTests` now that the monitor correctly refuses AX work when trust is absent.
+**Summary:** Delivered the permission and app-shell layer around the dictation skeleton. Ghost Pepper now starts through the documented permission sequence, exposes the onboarding-only and post-onboarding dropdown shells, reuses its shell windows, tracks launch-at-login and update-checking state, and recovers safely from corrupted shell-side storage. During wrap-up, later-dependent stories were moved forward instead of being marked done prematurely: STORY-0599 to ITER-0004, STORY-0605 and STORY-0606 to ITER-0005, STORY-0854 to ITER-0007, STORY-0038, STORY-0047, and STORY-0838 to ITER-0011, STORY-0040, STORY-0046, and STORY-0048 to ITER-0012, STORY-0840, STORY-0841, and STORY-0842 to ITER-0013, and STORY-0034 to ITER-0016.
